title: Running / ledger balance
template: SUM(CASE WHEN txn_type = 'credit' THEN amount ELSE -amount END) OVER (PARTITION BY account_id ORDER BY txn_date ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) as running_balance
anti_patterns: using RANGE instead of ROWS — causes ties on same date to produce same balance mid-day
not handling debit/credit sign convention consistently

title: Month-over-month and year-over-year growth
template: SELECT period, revenue, LAG(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY period) as prev_period, (revenue - LAG(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY period)) * 1.0 / NULLIF(LAG(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY period), 0) as mom_growth, LAG(revenue, 12) OVER (ORDER BY period) as same_period_last_year, (revenue - LAG(revenue, 12) OVER (ORDER BY period)) * 1.0 / NULLIF(LAG(revenue, 12) OVER (ORDER BY period), 0) as yoy_growth FROM monthly_revenue
anti_patterns: using LAG without explicit ORDER BY — result is non-deterministic
comparing unequal-length periods (28-day Feb vs 31-day March)

title: Budget vs actual variance
template: SELECT a.period, a.department, a.actual_amount, b.budget_amount, a.actual_amount - b.budget_amount as variance, (a.actual_amount - b.budget_amount) * 1.0 / NULLIF(b.budget_amount, 0) as variance_pct FROM actuals a LEFT JOIN budget b ON a.period = b.period AND a.department = b.department
anti_patterns: INNER JOIN drops departments with budget but no actuals — use LEFT JOIN
not normalising currency when budgets are in different currencies

title: Profit and loss summary
template: WITH pl AS (SELECT account_category, SUM(amount) as total FROM journal_entries WHERE period = :period GROUP BY 1) SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN account_category = 'revenue' THEN total ELSE 0 END) as revenue, SUM(CASE WHEN account_category = 'cogs' THEN total ELSE 0 END) as cogs, SUM(CASE WHEN account_category = 'revenue' THEN total ELSE 0 END) - SUM(CASE WHEN account_category = 'cogs' THEN total ELSE 0 END) as gross_profit, SUM(CASE WHEN account_category = 'opex' THEN total ELSE 0 END) as opex FROM pl
anti_patterns: mixing accrual and cash basis entries without filtering by accounting_basis

title: Gross margin by product / segment
template: SELECT product_id, SUM(revenue) as revenue, SUM(cogs) as cogs, SUM(revenue) - SUM(cogs) as gross_profit, (SUM(revenue) - SUM(cogs)) * 1.0 / NULLIF(SUM(revenue), 0) as gross_margin_pct FROM sales GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY gross_margin_pct DESC
anti_patterns: using unit cost instead of total COGS — misses volume effects

title: Accounts receivable aging
template: SELECT customer_id, invoice_id, invoice_amount, due_date, CURRENT_DATE - due_date as days_overdue, CASE WHEN CURRENT_DATE <= due_date THEN 'current' WHEN CURRENT_DATE - due_date <= 30 THEN '1-30 days' WHEN CURRENT_DATE - due_date <= 60 THEN '31-60 days' WHEN CURRENT_DATE - due_date <= 90 THEN '61-90 days' ELSE '90+ days' END as aging_bucket FROM invoices WHERE status != 'paid'
anti_patterns: using invoice_date instead of due_date for aging — overstates overdue amounts

title: Cash flow summary (operating / investing / financing)
template: SELECT period, SUM(CASE WHEN flow_type = 'operating' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) as operating, SUM(CASE WHEN flow_type = 'investing' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) as investing, SUM(CASE WHEN flow_type = 'financing' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) as financing, SUM(amount) as net_cash_flow FROM cash_transactions GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1
anti_patterns: mixing payment_date and accrual_date — always use cash settlement date for cash flow

title: Multi-currency conversion to base currency
template: SELECT t.transaction_id, t.amount, t.currency, t.amount * r.rate as amount_usd FROM transactions t LEFT JOIN fx_rates r ON t.currency = r.from_currency AND r.rate_date = DATE_TRUNC('day', t.transaction_date)
anti_patterns: using a single average rate for the period — use transaction-date rate for accuracy
LEFT JOIN without checking for NULL rate — missing FX rate silently produces NULL revenue

title: Days sales outstanding (DSO)
template: SELECT period, SUM(ending_ar) / NULLIF(SUM(revenue) / 30.0, 0) as dso FROM ar_summary GROUP BY 1
anti_patterns: using beginning AR instead of ending AR
dividing by daily revenue of the same period without annualising

title: Period-end close balance (opening + activity + closing)
template: WITH period_activity AS (SELECT account_id, SUM(CASE WHEN txn_date < :period_start THEN amount ELSE 0 END) as opening_balance, SUM(CASE WHEN txn_date BETWEEN :period_start AND :period_end THEN amount ELSE 0 END) as period_activity FROM transactions GROUP BY 1) SELECT account_id, opening_balance, period_activity, opening_balance + period_activity as closing_balance FROM period_activity
anti_patterns: using two separate queries for opening and closing — risk of inconsistent filters

title: Revenue recognition (ratable / straight-line)
template: WITH months AS (SELECT DISTINCT DATE_TRUNC('month', d) as month FROM generate_series(:start, :end, INTERVAL '1 month') d) SELECT m.month, s.contract_id, s.contract_value / (EXTRACT(MONTH FROM AGE(s.end_date, s.start_date)) + 1) as recognised_revenue FROM months m JOIN subscriptions s ON m.month BETWEEN DATE_TRUNC('month', s.start_date) AND DATE_TRUNC('month', s.end_date)
anti_patterns: booking full contract value in month of signing instead of spreading across contract term

title: Expense ratio and cost breakdown
template: SELECT category, SUM(amount) as total_expense, SUM(amount) * 1.0 / SUM(SUM(amount)) OVER () as expense_share, SUM(amount) * 1.0 / NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN type='revenue' THEN amount END) OVER (), 0) as expense_to_revenue FROM expenses GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 2 DESC
anti_patterns: mixing capex and opex in the same expense_to_revenue ratio
